question: Design a `Department` class that holds a collection of `Professor` objects (aggregation). Implement methods to `hireProfessor()`, `fireProfessor()`, and `displayProfessors()`.

test_cases.expected:
Hired Professor: Alice Smith (ID: 101)
Hired Professor: Bob Johnson (ID: 102)
Hired Professor: Charlie Brown (ID: 103)

--- Current Professors ---
Department: Computer Science
  Professor ID: 101, Name: Alice Smith
  Professor ID: 102, Name: Bob Johnson
  Professor ID: 103, Name: Charlie Brown

--- Firing Professor 102 (Bob) ---
Attempting to fire Professor with ID: 102
Fired a professor (incorrectly, always the last one, which was Charlie Brown ID: 103).

--- Professors After Firing ---
Department: Computer Science
  Professor ID: 101, Name: Alice Smith
  Professor ID: 102, Name: Bob Johnson

--- Trying to fire non-existent Professor 999 ---
Attempting to fire Professor with ID: 999
Fired a professor (incorrectly, always the last one, which was Bob Johnson ID: 102).

--- Final Professors ---
Department: Computer Science
  Professor ID: 101, Name: Alice Smith

errors: The `fireProfessor` method incorrectly removes the last professor from the collection using `pop_back()` instead of finding and removing the professor with the specified ID. This leads to incorrect state management as the wrong professor is removed.
